Overview

About This Book

The ETS ParaPro Assessment is required for paraprofessional certification in most US school districts. It tests Reading, Writing, and Mathematics at a basic skills level — but the combination of subjects and the test format trips up many candidates. Maxwell Pepper's ParaPro Assessment Study Guide wraps all the content in a Hamlet-inspired narrative that makes the review engaging rather than exhausting, with 2 full-length practice tests to confirm you are ready for exam day.

What This Guide Covers

  1. Reading — Content Knowledge (18 questions)
  2. Reading — Application to Classroom (12 questions)
  3. Writing — Content Knowledge (18 questions)
  4. Writing — Application to Classroom (12 questions)
  5. Mathematics — Content Knowledge (18 questions)
  6. Mathematics — Application to Classroom (12 questions)
Get It on Amazon ↗
Topics

What's Covered

Reading — Content Knowledge (18 questions)

Identifying main ideas, understanding vocabulary in context, recognizing text structure, and making inferences.

Reading — Application to Classroom (12 questions)

Applying reading skills to classroom instruction: helping students comprehend, identify literary elements, and use context clues.

Writing — Content Knowledge (18 questions)

Grammar, usage, sentence structure, punctuation, capitalization, and editing skills.

Writing — Application to Classroom (12 questions)

Supporting student writing: identifying errors, suggesting improvements, and applying writing instruction strategies.

Mathematics — Content Knowledge (18 questions)

Number sense, operations, measurement, geometry, data analysis, and basic algebra.

Mathematics — Application to Classroom (12 questions)

Applying math skills to classroom contexts: explaining procedures, identifying student errors, and supporting math instruction.

Is This For You?

Who This Book Helps

  • ✓Paraprofessional candidates required to pass the ParaPro for employment or certification
  • ✓Teaching assistants and instructional aides seeking to advance in their role
  • ✓Candidates who need to brush up on Reading, Writing, and Math before the exam
  • ✓Anyone who has struggled with traditional test prep and wants a more engaging format

Why This Book Is Different

Most ParaPro prep guides are straightforward review books — useful but forgettable. The Hamlet-inspired narrative gives you a story thread to follow while reviewing all three content areas, making the material easier to retain and the study sessions more enjoyable. Two full-length practice tests at the end confirm readiness before you walk into the testing center.

FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

How many questions are on the ParaPro Assessment?
The ParaPro Assessment contains 90 multiple-choice questions: 30 each in Reading, Writing, and Mathematics. Each section contains 18 content knowledge questions and 12 application-to-classroom questions.
What is a passing score on the ParaPro?
ParaPro passing scores vary by state and employer. ETS reports scores on a scale from 420 to 480. Check with your school district or state education agency for the specific passing score required in your area.
How long is the ParaPro exam?
The ParaPro Assessment is 2.5 hours (150 minutes) in length. It is administered at Prometric testing centers.
How hard is the ParaPro?
The ParaPro tests basic skills at roughly a 10th-12th grade level. Most candidates who prepare adequately pass on their first attempt. The math section is the most challenging area for many candidates.
Is a calculator allowed on the ParaPro?
No. Calculators are not permitted on the ParaPro Assessment. The math section tests arithmetic, basic algebra, and geometry without the aid of a calculator.
